name: Resizable Intent
summary: A standalone linear-split divider — grab a separator between two panes and resize them, recursable (Fork 1 of #1384, the spatial-manipulation placement). The 'linear split' layout model: a 1-D draggable divider whose drag reflows its sibling, no collision. Any block + the layout intent's `pane` compose it. Carries the APG Window Splitter a11y contract as a fixed invariant (role=separator + aria-valuenow/min/max + arrow/Home/End keys), over a Pointer Events substrate. Distinct from arrangeable (2-D grid placement) and from a recursive partition/dock tree (deferred, #1437).

orientation.description: Which axis the divider runs along — `vertical` is a vertical divider that resizes left/right panes (the common side-by-side split); `horizontal` resizes top/bottom panes. Most-permissive default: absent → inferred from the pane layout's main axis.

unit.description: How the divider position is expressed and persisted — `percent` keeps the split ratio stable across container resizes (the sensible default for responsive panes); `pixel` fixes one pane's size and lets the other absorb the slack.

collapse.description: Whether a pane can collapse fully to its edge — `none` clamps to the pane's min size; `edge` lets the divider snap the pane closed at an edge (and a double-click / keyboard toggle restores it), the collapsible-sidebar pattern. Most-permissive default: `none`.

description:
What This Is

The linear-split layout model (Fork 1 of #1384): a 1-D draggable divider between two panes, recursable. Resizing one pane reflows its sibling; there is no collision or free placement (that is arrangeable). The layout intent already names a pane as a "resizable subdivision" but ships no divider — this intent is that divider, composed by layout.pane and by any block that subdivides space.

Dimensions

  • orientation: vertical (resizes left/right) vs horizontal (resizes top/bottom).
  • unit: percent (stable ratio across container resize — default) vs pixel (fixed size on one pane).
  • collapse: none (clamp to min — default) vs edge (snap a pane closed at its edge, restorable).

Per-pane min / max size constraints are numeric properties of the binding (not an enum axis) — they bound the divider's travel and feed aria-valuemin/aria-valuemax.

Fixed a11y invariant — APG Window Splitter

Keyboard operability is part of the contract, not an enhancement. Borrowed verbatim from the WAI-ARIA APG Window Splitter pattern: the divider is role="separator" with aria-valuenow/aria-valuemin/aria-valuemax and aria-controls; Arrow keys move it by a step, Home/End jump to min/max, Enter toggles a collapse. (react-resizable-panels and react-split-pane are the rare incumbents that ship this; most do not — the gap WE closes.) Because it is a fixed invariant, it is not a dimension.

Substrate & composition

Built on Pointer Events (unified mouse/touch/pen, pointer capture) with touch-action: none so the browser doesn't consume the drag. The pointer drag itself is recognized via the gesture intent (pan); the divider is the recognition's effect. Composes layout.pane (the subdivision it resizes). Grounded in the spatial-manipulation survey (spatial-manipulation arrangeable surfaces).
